mnemonics.title: Five Dimensions of IxD
mnemonics.content: WWV-PTB: Words, Visual representations, Physical objects/space, Time, Behavior (WWV-PTB = Wonder Woman Visits Peter's Two Brothers)

mnemonics.title: Usability Goals
mnemonics.content: SEE-SLUM: Safety, Effectiveness, Efficiency, Satisfaction, Learnability, Utility, Memorability (SEE = see the product works, SLUM = slowly learn using memory)

mnemonics.title: User Story Format
mnemonics.content: ASW: As a [user], So that [benefit], I want [goal] - Remember: A S W = Ask See Want

acronyms.usage: A methodology that places users at the center of all design decisions throughout the development process
The discipline of designing interactive products and systems that facilitate meaningful user experiences
The overall experience a user has when interacting with a product, including emotions, perceptions, and responses
The structural design of information and content organization within digital products
A question users ask to understand the personal benefit of a feature or product
Key elements to consider when planning user research projects

visualTips.visualization: Create a profile card with a photo (use stock image), name, job title, goals (green), frustrations (red), and quote - imagine meeting this person face-to-face
Draw a horizontal timeline with emoji faces showing emotions (😊→😟→😊) at each touchpoint to visualize the complete user experience
Use swimlane diagrams with decision diamonds (◇) and action rectangles (□) connected by arrows showing user flows from start to goal
Use sticky notes on a wall, grouped by colored dots - imagine clustering similar Post-it notes to find patterns like organizing photos into albums

analogies.analogy: Like a character in a movie script - just as actors need character profiles to understand their role, designers use personas to understand user characters
Like a detective gathering clues before solving a case - you must observe, interview, and gather evidence about users before designing solutions
Like a recipe - just as ingredients and steps guide cooking, requirements guide design, ensuring all necessary elements are included
Like understanding the setting of a play - you can't design a costume without knowing if it's set in space, ancient Rome, or modern day
Like a library's organization system - just as books are categorized by subject, content is organized so users can find what they need
